title: Marine Rifleman - Chosin Reservoir, 1950
startLocation: Hagaru-ri perimeter, Chosin Reservoir, North Korea
opening: Twenty below zero and the wind comes off the reservoir like a saw blade. Your canteen froze solid an hour ago; the Tootsie Rolls in your pocket are the only ration soft enough to eat. Gunny Mercer kicks boots down the line of fighting holes - 'wiggle your toes, ladies, dead toes don't march' - and squints north at the ridgelines across the ice. Headquarters says the Chinese aren't in this fight in strength. The old salts who fought at Guadalcanal look at the silent hills and say nothing at all. Tonight the moon will be bright on the snow. Listen: somewhere far off, a bugle.

squad.name: Gunny Ray Mercer
squad.trait: Pacific veteran, profanity as poetry, checks every man's feet nightly

squad.name: Cpl. Bill Cho
squad.trait: Korean-American from Los Angeles, the platoon's voice and conscience

squad.name: Pfc. Tommy Two Bears
squad.trait: Lakota, jokes only when things are worst, never misses

squad.name: Pfc. Artie Greene
squad.trait: draftee fresh from boot camp, asks too many questions, learning fast

squad.name: Doc Petrillo
squad.trait: keeps morphine syrettes in his mouth so they won't freeze

timelinePressure: 1950-11-27 The Chinese Ninth Army Group attacks across the Chosin front by night
1950-11-28 Fox Company holds Toktong Pass alone
1950-12-01 The breakout begins - 'attacking in a different direction'
1950-12-11 The column reaches Hungnam; the fleet waits offshore
